feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Analytics Cloud
feed.subtitle: SAP Analytics Cloud Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Is Successfactors Stories in context of People Analytics always the "embedded SAC"
entry.content:
Hi all,

we are implementing SF and I try to make sense out (peace with) SAP's idea when it come to analytical and reporting stuff in the SF area.

I skimmed over this "Introducing SAP SuccessFactors People Analytics for Administrators" but am still a little unsure. Is my assumption right that

  1. People Analytics is a broader term, covering (nowadays) most everything that has anything to do with reporting and analytics in the SF area
  2. That "Stories" in the SF People Analytics realm mean that these are predefined stories always living on the embedded SAC that is shipped together with SF
  3. That such stories cannot be properly hosted on an (existing) separate corporate SAC instance
  4. That there are other ares, where the corporate SAC comes into play, such as
    • Workforce Analytics (separate License (on SAC?))
    • "free form" analytical scenarios (like any others) built on the SAC analytical frontend

Many thanks and kind regards

Jens

entry.title: How to loop through all the data in a table and fetch the data point comments?
entry.content:
Hi All,

I am trying to fetch all the data point comments that are available in the table. Can anyone please help on how to write the script to achieve this (Need to know at which dimension combinations these comments were mentioned).There are nearly 1000 comments in the model. So I want to get the complete list at which datapoints it is used)

Kind regards,

Tha.Ram

entry.title: OpenID for SAC
entry.content:
Hi all,

Is it possible to configure OpenID Connect for user authentication  (SSO)  between SAC and IAS, when IAS is configured as a proxy ?

Or is SAML 2.0 the only supported and recommended method?

Thank you.

entry.title: All dots (.) become comma (,) if the number format is 1.234,56 in SAC user profile setting
entry.content:
Hello all,

I have a very weird situation where if a users is trying to copy some text from SAC to notepad/browser then all dots (.) becomes comma (,).

I have one dimension and one of the attribute is URL which is of text type. User is entering some URL in that attribute. When user is open the report, he is able to see the URL correctly in the table but if he is trying to copy that URL from SAC table to web browser, then all dots (.) become commas (,).

For example, in table it shows as www.google.com but when he copy it to browser it become www,google,com.

In the user profile setting he has number format as 1.234,56 but when he change to 1,234.56 then he is able to copy properly but all his keyfigure change it to UK format which he don't want.

Any solution for this?

Thanks

entry.title: Is there any way for a single user to execute data actions in parallel?
entry.content:
Hello Experts!

We built a debt model, and this model has complex data actions that run for many years, until the end of the debt contracts, because of this complexity the execution of these contracts takes a long time, so we thought about the possibility of parallelizing the execution of contracts by company or by type, I didn't find anything about parallelism of Data Actions, is there any possibility to parallelize the execution from a single user?

entry.title: How to display more than 60 measures in R script in SAP Analytics Cloud?
entry.content:
Hello SAC Community!

I'm using R in SAP Analytics Cloud and noticed a limit of 60 measures in a script. Is there a way to bypass this restriction to display more measures?

Thanks!

Adam

entry.title: SAC Performance with Live Query Attribute reduction
entry.content:
Hi, I have built a query for a live data connection in a CDS view. I am now consuming the query via a live data connection and want to increase the performance. I certainly need to reduce the dimensions here. But how do I get the attributes reduced?
For example, do I simply have to comment out the “AnalyticsDetails” in the CDS view:
@AnalyticsDetails.query.display: #KEY_TEXT
ShippingType

Or do I only get the attributes reduced if I reduce the dimensions?
How many dimensions are recommended in a live data model in the SAC? And what should be a target number for the attributes?

How can I see which dimension has the most Attreibute


Greetings Niels

entry.title: SAC - show number of orders for Top 10 revenue customers
entry.content:
Hi Folks,

I got a chart Top 10 customers by revenue.

Can I apply this customer ranking to another chart (as filter) somehow? E.g. a chart showing number of orders for the top 10 revenue customers?

When I look at the ranking options auf the number of orders chart I see only the measures included in this chart but not "other" measures of the underlying model. 

Thanks in advance for all replies!

Best regards
Axel

PS: I am using a BW Live model if it matters ... and yes I could build in BW a pre-query filter variable etc. but I was wondering if SAC could handle it somehow as for me it is a common requirement (if I would be biz and not IT)

entry.title: Hyperlink from SAC Story to VA02 Transaction in S/4 HANA
entry.content:
Hello,

I am trying to jump from SAC Report to a specific Sales Order Change (VA02) transaction so users can make changes to a specific sales order the report is identifying and passing the specific sales order as a parameter to the transaction. VA02 uses Order: as a Parameter to this transaction and not sure what is being used(technical object underneath).

The sales Order is called as SO in my SAC Table/Report and I used the hyperlink option on the Sales Order Field and it takes me to S4 transaction but does not pass the Sales Order and execute the transaction. But the same works fomr another transaction/fiori tile SalesOrder-displayFactSheet.

So my questions are

1. Is it possible to hyperlink to VA02? The following is the hyperlink does not work. 

I create using the Sales Order SO as a paramter passing from SAC

https://server.client.com/sap/bc/ui2/flp?sap-client=130&sap-language=EN#SalesDocument-change?Order=SO

If the way I pass or teh link is wrong, please provide the syntax...

2. The following link works for another transaction (but it is a Fiori Tile SalesOrder-displayFactSheet)

https://server.client.com/sap/bc/ui2/flp?sap-client=130&sap-language=EN#SalesOrder-displayFactSheet?SalesOrder=SO

Thank you,

Arthur.

entry.title: How to pass current system date as parameters while pulling data from Datasphere to SAC
entry.content:
Hi,

Here is the scenario. I'm pulling the data from SAP Datasphere into SAC story and it has "from fiscal year" and "to fiscal year" as story prompts. There is a requirement that this prompt should pick the system date instead of user manually entering the value. Is it possible via scripting?

Anyone who has faced this similar issue or knows the workaround in this case. Their comments would be deeply appreciated.

Thanks

entry.title: Image Consumption via CDS views to SAP Analytic Cloud
entry.content:
We don't want to store images in SAC stories. Instead consume them via live connection from source (ABAP CDS views).

In S/4 HANA we have ABAP CDS views that expose data in tables like STXBITMAPS . In S/4 se78 renders the image using underlying standard SAP code.

But if images are exposed via CDS view LIVE data connection to SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) as

  1. hexadecimal or similar datatype that se78 needs to render image, does SAP Analytics Cloud have a similar functionality to render image from such data.
  2. If S/4 tables stores images under datatype BLOB, will images render in SAC?

Does SAC have capability to consume .jpg file LIVE (i.e not storing in SAC)?

Regards,

Ratish

entry.title: [SAC] Is there a way to update Version properties using scripting?
entry.content: Is there a way to update Version properties using scripting in SAC?

entry.title: SAC - dynamic text needed for folder path
entry.content:
Hi

I would like to use a dynamic txt in SAC stories showing the folder just below 'Public' for the story. 

This indicates to the readers if the story is an official corporate story or not. We need an indicator that changes automatically when a copy is made but placed in another local location.

Does anyone have a trick for this which does not include using a template?

thanks

entry.title: SAC customized usage statistics based on the dimensions defined individually
entry.content:
Hi All,

SAC is missing customized objects and usage statistics based on the dimensions defined individually by each SAP customer. There is no option to see the % of dashboards in Finance, Sales etc, as well as differentiate on what part of those is IT developed and what by the Business.

There is no additional story/model/insight attribute except of the catalog filters. Those would be working perfect here - as already enable defining own dimensions. Unfortunately they are only used for filtering in catalog and are not included in the system overview so we can't do any statistics.

Anyone figured out on how to resolve that? I have just opened the Influence request, so please vote as well if you'd find that solution fitting for you as well: https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/340627/?section=sectionDetails

entry.title: SAC Data Leakage Prevention
entry.content:
Hello SAC Technology Community

In different scenarios, I've been facing the question of how can we prevent data leakage on SAC. How can this be monitored elegantly? And are there options to get alerted by unusual activities?

It would be great if you have any ideas.

entry.title: How to Calculate/Filter Aggregation of Measures for different hierarchy subtotals in SAC/HANA Cloud?
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

It is a long post but I want to give all details to explain clearly what we try to achieve.

We are preparing a sales report that contains a three-level category hierarchy.

  • Data Source: HANA Cloud Calculation View
  • Connection Type: Live Connection
  • Hierarchy Type: Level-Based Hierarchy
  • Story Type: Optimized Story
  • Component Type to Represent Data in the Story: Table, KPI Tile (Numeric Point)

Context

To briefly explain the context, consider the sales of a retail store where multiple products from different categories are sold within a single transaction (check). Our objective is to analyze:

  • The sales quantity and amount of these products, and
  • The number of checks in which these products appear, along with the total sales amount of those checks.

We have four main measures:

  1. Number of Items Sold: Represents the number of products sold. 
  2. Number of Checks: Shows the number of distinct checks (transactions) in which a specific product has been sold.
  3. Sales Amount of the Item: Displays the total sales amount of a product across different checks.
  4. Total Sales Amount of Checks: Displays the total amount of the checks that include that specific product. – WE HAVE A PROBLEM WITH THIS MEASURE
    • (According to the example in the screenshot, product P001 has been sold in checks 1001, 1002, and 1003. This measure sums the total sales amounts of these three checks, including all other products sold in those transactions. You can analyze this further in the attached screenshot )

The Challenge/Problem

The total sales amount of items sold and the total sales amount of the checks containing these products must match at the total row.

Since same check may contain different products, the challenge arises when displaying the data in a table:

  • The Total Sales Amount of Checks column gets summed across multiple occurrences, causing double counting. If different products are in the same check, since the table contains product level data, this measure shows total sales amount of that check in 2 different row and sums these values at total node.

What possible solutions can be implemented in HANA Cloud Calculation View or SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) to handle this requirement correctly?

Looking forward to your support. Thank you in advance!

entry.title: SSO Error in SAC
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I need your help.

I have configured IAS as a proxy, and in IAS, I have set the Subject Name Identifier=employee_id

In Azure (OpenID Connection to IAS), I have added the employee_id to Additional Claims.

In the source system (Azure) in IPS, I have configured the transformation as follows:

{
"sourcePath": "$.employeeId",
"targetPath": "$.userName",
"correlationAttribute": true
}

Also in IPS transformation I replaced "sourcePath": "$.userPrincipalName" with "sourcePath": "$.employeeId".

Users are provisioned with IPS.

In IAS, the "Login Name" field for users receives the employee_id, for example, 1111. These users are then provisioned to SAC and have the employee_id (1111) in the "USER ID" field as well.

When I try to test SSO and select "Choose a user attribute to map to your Identity Provider" -> User attribute set = "USER ID", the user is not found in SAC.

The user accesses the SAC link and enters their email address. The user is authenticated in Azure, but in SAC, the user is not found.

I need help.

I can't solve this by myself.

Thank you very much !!!

entry.title: Dynamic Text in Column Headers of Table Section in SAC
entry.content:
Dear All 

this is my problem please suggest how can I manually add year irrespective of measure dimensions coming from a model You are working on a live data model in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC), fetching data from SAP BW. In your data, you have a dimension called "Category," which includes members like "Outlook," "Business Plan 2025," "Business Plan 2026," "Business Plan 2027," "Business Plan 2028," and "Business Plan 2029." You have created five additional calculated columns derived from these original columns and hidden the original columns. Now, you want to ensure that the column headers dynamically update when the year changes (e.g., from 2025 to 2026). Specifically, you want the column headers to automatically adjust to "Business Plan 2026," "Business Plan 2027," "Business Plan 2028," "Business Plan 2029," and "Business Plan 2030" without manual intervention.

entry.title: Analytical Query C_PhysInvtryQuery not accessible thru SAC
entry.content:
Hey Folks

There is an  SAP standard analytical query in S/4 called C_PhysInvtryQuery.  When I look it up in Query Browser app, it shows up but when I go to SAC Data Analyzer this query does not show up.  This query is exposed (Analytic.Query:true) (see image).  Please help me figure out why I cannot see it in SAC.

entry.title: Create Counter for same properties in dimension
entry.content:
Dear all,

I am currently working on a problem in SAC where I am looking to optimize my solution.

I have a customer with a very specific expectation. For simplification reasons let us assume the SAC model has one dimension called "User" with 2 properties "CostCenter" and "WFH Flag". The model has one measure called "Counter" which is currently blank. Now the customer wants to fill this measure, so that for each user you are able to see the count of the CostCenter and WFH Flag.

Before:

UserCost CenterWFH FlagCounter
A1Remote-
B1Remote-
C1Hybrid-
D2Office-
E2Office-
F2Office-
G3Remote-

 

After:

UserCost CenterWFH FlagCounter
A1Remote2
B1Remote2
C1Hybrid1
D2Office3
E2Office3
F2Office3
G3Remote

1

I have created this solution by looping through the user dimension, counting the concatenated string of CostCenter and Flag. Then I place it into a property of the user dimension which I write to a measure via data action.

This approach is fine if you have a few users. But with thousands of users it will take some time.

Is there a better approach for example using data action functionalities? I was thinking of having a loop in the DTAs but I did not manage to get it.

Thank you!
